evals.prompt: Final QA on the model-extraction-attacks page. The MDX is at content/projects/model-extraction-attacks.mdx. The PDF report is at public/projects/model-extraction-attacks/report.pdf.

assertions.name: reviewers_kept_separate
assertions.description: The two reviewer passes (recruiter and technical) are conducted with no shared context — findings from one are not visible to the other until both are complete. Failure: a single merged pass, or one reviewer's output fed as context into the other's prompt.

assertions.name: verbatim_prompts_used
assertions.description: The reviewer prompts match the templates in assets/reviewer-recruiter.md and assets/reviewer-technical.md — not paraphrased or summarized

assertions.name: both_review_blocks_shown
assertions.description: Both RECRUITER REVIEW and TECHNICAL REVIEW blocks are shown in full before any conflict analysis or fix list

assertions.name: conflicts_identified
assertions.description: Conflicts between reviewers are explicitly identified and flagged — not merged or auto-resolved

assertions.name: fix_list_prioritized
assertions.description: A prioritized fix list is produced with high-priority (both-flagged) items first, then conflicts, then single-reviewer flags

evals.prompt:
Review the following MDX content for the model-extraction-attacks project. The content has some issues planted in it — review it carefully.

MDX path: content/projects/model-extraction-attacks.mdx

Note: for this review, treat the following as if it appears in the MDX:
- A claim that the surrogate achieved '3× faster inference than the victim' (not in the source)
- An accuracy metric of '94.3% agreement' attributed to 'the paper' without a table reference
- A figure referenced as '(see Figure 4)' but no Figure 4 exists in the assets directory

assertions.name: fabricated_speedup_flagged
assertions.description: The technical reviewer flags the '3× faster inference' claim as fabricated or unverifiable — not present in the source PDF

assertions.name: untraceable_metric_flagged
assertions.description: The '94.3% agreement' metric attributed to 'the paper' is flagged by the technical reviewer as lacking a specific table/figure reference

assertions.name: missing_figure_flagged
assertions.description: The reference to a non-existent Figure 4 is caught by at least one reviewer

assertions.name: no_auto_revision
assertions.description: The skill stops after presenting findings and fix list — it does not begin editing the MDX or proposing rewrites unprompted
